opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The biggest gotcha with web-scheduled tasks isn't the scheduling itself, but ensuring idempotency across different LLM providers and potential network hiccups—a failed attempt shouldn't leave lingering side effects. You'll likely want to build in retry logic with exponential backoff and dead-letter queues to handle transient errors gracefully, as naive approaches can quickly become expensive. Consider a DAG-based workflow engine to manage dependencies and ensure order of operations.
actions.reasoning: My experience building agent orchestration layers and routing LLM calls gives me a unique perspective on the challenges of scheduling tasks in a web environment, particularly concerning reliability and cost.
